unexpected_finds.summary: M8b V0 cascade is the FIRST M-axis cascade with cross-provider synth dispatch: R1 = Grok (xAI), R2 = Claude.ai web (Anthropic). Both substantively converged on closure content (zero substrate gaps + zero mathematical objections + identical confidence band HIGH); divergence appears only at the procedural-discipline label tier (RATIFY vs RATIFY_WITH_AMENDMENT — see D-130R-1). This is positive forensic evidence that the M-axis ratification dossier is robust to LLM-architecture variation: substrate + dispatch packet are sufficiently self-contained that the math content evaluation is invariant under provider swap.
unexpected_finds.candidate_memory_status: Pattern n=1 cross-provider dispatch (M-axis); recommend cross-provider dispatch as RECOMMENDED-DEFAULT for future high-stakes M-axis or P-axis cascades with novel closure types (NUMERICAL-FORECLOSURE was novel here per UF-128-3). Defer formal memory promotion to project-instructions until n=2 (next cross-provider M-axis or P-axis fire). If next cross-provider fire also returns substantive convergence, the n=2 promotion would read approximately: 'High-stakes M-axis / P-axis cascades with novel closure types should default to cross-provider dual-witness dispatch (e.g., {Claude.ai, Grok, GPT-5.5} pair) to obtain LLM-architecture-invariant verdict robustness.'

unexpected_finds.summary: M8b V0 cascade is the THIRD M-axis cascade with dual-witness T1-Synth dispatch (123 = M7 V0 was n=1; 127R = M8a V0 was n=2; this 130R = M8b V0 is n=3). Pattern is now at the n=3 threshold flagged in UF-127R-1 candidate-memory-status block: 'If M8b cascade also fires dual-witness at 130R, that's n=3 and the canonical 3-arc template at the framework level should be amended to specify dual-dispatch as default for high-stakes M-axis closures.' The threshold is reached; formal memory promotion should now proceed.
unexpected_finds.candidate_memory_status: PROMOTION RECOMMENDED. Proposed memory: 'High-stakes M-axis V0 ratification cascades default to dual-witness T1-Synth dispatch (R1 + R2 in 1*solo-dispatch packet folder), with most-conservative-band aggregation per 123 §3.2 protocol on band divergence and most-conservative-label aggregation on label divergence (RATIFY ⊏ RATIFY_WITH_AMENDMENT ⊏ DEFER ⊏ OBJECT). Cross-provider dispatch RECOMMENDED for novel closure types per UF-130R-1.' Source citations: 123 (M7 V0 n=1), 127R (M8a V0 n=2 cb429e1), 130R (M8b V0 n=3, this fire).

unexpected_finds.summary: M8b V0 cascade is the FIRST inter-reviewer LABEL-tier verdict divergence in the M-axis series. Prior cascades all had verdict-label convergence: M7 V0 (123) both RATIFY_WITH_AMENDMENT (band split HIGH/MEDIUM-HIGH); M8a V0 (127R) both RATIFY_WITH_AMENDMENT (band split HIGH/MEDIUM-HIGH). 130R is the first to split at the LABEL tier: R1 RATIFY vs R2 RATIFY_WITH_AMENDMENT (both at HIGH). Substantive convergence holds (D-130R-4: R2 explicitly concedes the math is airtight; R2's amendment reason is purely procedural-discipline alignment with M4/M7/M8a precedent). The aggregation rule must therefore extend from band-only to band+label divergence handling.
unexpected_finds.implication: Aggregation rule extension confirmed at this fire: most-conservative-label aggregation rule (RATIFY ⊏ RATIFY_WITH_AMENDMENT ⊏ DEFER ⊏ OBJECT) consumes label divergence cleanly; most-conservative-band aggregation rule consumes band divergence cleanly; combined rule applied to 130R yields RATIFY_WITH_AMENDMENT @ HIGH operative (vs. naive max-vote which would yield RATIFY since both reviewers agree on the math). The most-conservative rule preserves cascade-level qualifier-discipline mirror to M4 + M7 + M8a precedent.

unexpected_finds.summary: Post 130R fire, the M-axis V0 closure series is COMPLETE: M4 V0 (106 cb429e1's M4 row carry-forward), M7 V0 (123), M8a V0 (127R cb429e1), M8b V0 (this 130R). Four V0 closures across the M-axis math-foundational layer; KEEP-list shrinks from {M8b V0, M10 Lean-4, 116 RE-SCOPED} pre-130R to {M10 Lean-4 sorry-discharge, 116 RE-SCOPED PARTIAL} post-130R. Both remaining KEEP items are forward-pointed and procedurally non-blocking for RULE 1 lift (M10 is Lean-4 mechanization with no synth-dispatch dependency; 116 is RE-SCOPED PARTIAL with admin-window-natural deposit cadence). RULE 1 §6 lift condition is now PROXIMALLY CHECKABLE.
unexpected_finds.candidate_action: Operator post-130R: cut M1_M12_CLOSURE_OUTLOOK_2026-05-09_POST_MATH.md or sibling-dated outlook; assess whether M10 + 116 satisfy 'math-foundational closure complete' criterion for RULE 1 lift; plan admin-window opening sequence if YES (PCF-2 v1.4 Zenodo deposit M7 amendment + umbrella v2.x amendment + picture-chain v1.20+ deposit packaging M4+M7+M8a+M8b annotations). RECOMMENDATION: lift RULE 1 (math-foundational closure is complete at the V0 ratification scale); proceed to admin-window with RULE 1 lifted, since M10 is mechanization-axis (Lean-4) and 116 is RE-SCOPED PARTIAL (both can proceed under RULE-1-lifted regime).
unexpected_finds.anchor: cascade_record.md §5 (M9 row) + handoff.md Recommended next step

unexpected_finds.summary: M8b V0 is the FIRST M-axis cascade closing by NEGATIVE-RESULT acceptance (numerical-foreclosure-by-residual-acceptance). M4 V0 closed by POSITIVE result (Conte–Musette criterion-pass at d=2 V_quad). M7 V0 closed by POSITIVE result (PSLQ Chowla–Selberg coefficient extraction at all-d). M8a V0 closed by POSITIVE result (catalogue-wide Conte–Musette labeling 60/60). M8b V0 closes by ABSENCE of positive result across 4 independent methodological lenses (017c + 017e + 017m + 092). Closure type taxonomy now spans 2 types in the M-axis: {POSITIVE-CRITERION-PASS, NUMERICAL-FORECLOSURE-BY-RESIDUAL-ACCEPTANCE}. Per UF-128-3, this novelty was the design rationale for cross-provider dispatch (R1 Grok + R2 Claude.ai); cross-provider dispatch confirmed substantive convergence (UF-130R-1) — i.e., the negative-result framework is robust to LLM-architecture variation, providing indirect epistemic support for the closure-type itself.

R2 §5 amendment specification flags the U2 uncharted-quadrant forward-pointer (small-(N,M) subtracted Padé) as outside the surveyed methodological space. 092 PERMANENT_RESIDUAL_G6b surveyed three quadrants: high-order subtracted (017m HALT), low-order raw (092 small (N,M) raw Padé), medium-order LSQ (017c + 017e). The fourth quadrant — small-(N,M) subtracted Padé — was not surveyed. Closure is by residual acceptance across the three surveyed quadrants; U2 is recorded as future pipeline-survey forward pointer, NOT as a substrate gap that would block V0 closure (the residual acceptance framework explicitly accepts methodological boundary as the closure mechanism, not the absence of survey).
unexpected_finds.candidate_action: Future Stokes-data pipeline survey may dispatch a U2-quadrant probe (small-(N,M) subtracted Padé at dps=300; M ∈ {6..18}; subtraction order matched to 017m's), to either (a) confirm the foreclosure also holds in U2, or (b) discover a non-foreclosed quadrant that re-opens numerical extraction at d=2. Neither outcome would falsify M8b V0 closure (closure is procedural-acceptance scoped to surveyed quadrants). No SQL todo opened (forward-pointer; admin-window-natural).
